calendar.matters:
Petition To Compel Trustee To Deliver Assets Of Surviving Spouse Trust And Demand For Accounting
Off Calendar Dec-06-2016 Granted Per Written Order

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Petition For Approval Of Trustees Fees
Off Calendar Dec-06-2016 Text Ruling

Petition To Compel Trustee To Deliver Assets Of Surviving Spouse Trust And Demand For Accounting
Off Calendar Sep-21-2016 Continued To Dec-05-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Boessenecker for (petitioner/objector) David Haslett. Amy Harrington for (trustee of the David Haslett trust) Franza Giffen. Daniel Conrad for and with (trustee of the family trust) Thomas Lucas.

The Court asked where the parties stand regarding the petition to compel. Mr. Boessenecker stated that the petition is intertwined with the accounting, gave the history of the request for a report regarding capacity, that Mr. Lucas was not satisfied with the report and requested that Dr. Canick review a 2000 page Kaiser report and that they want Mr. Lucas to pay for this, that they mis-stated that the fees to Mr. Conrad are set forth in the accounting, that they are only objecting to the attorney's fees regarding this issue of capacity, that there was a capital loss of $40,000.00 and that they want Mr. Lucas surcharged and would like a status date on the transferring of assets. Mr. Conrad stated that the conclusion of Dr. Canick's report is that Mr. Haslett is a little bit above the line, that they did not object to the report, that Mr. Lucas signed a letter to transfer the U.S. Bank and LLC interests to Ms. Giffen, that they have requested the deed to transfer the residence, that the position of Mr. Haslett and his attorney is that there was a refusal to transfer assets but that is false, counsel gave a background of the case and how Mr. Lucas was hired, that the wife died and in the midst of Mr. Haslett's grief he fired his attorney and trustee which is a red flag of undue influence, that Mr. Lucas' concerns were well founded, that Dr. Canick noted that prior to testing and review of the documents he thought Mr. Haslett did not have capacity, counsel referred to a letter from Mr. Siracusa to Ms. O'Dowd in which Ms. O'Dowd did not respond, that Mr. Lucas acted properly by protecting the trust, that his actions were part of his fiduciary duties, that 2/3 of the assets have been transferred and confirmed that everything should be done within 60 days, that Mr. Lucas has been paid nothing as trustee, that the loss in securities were securities that had belonged to the Haslett's and were sold as they were not performing, that Dr. Canick's bill is high as this is a complicated case and that Mr. Lucas has done nothing wrong. Mr. Boessenecker stated that they are not demonizing Mr. Lucas, that he agrees with Mr. Conrad in that these issues due raise red flags, that there was never clarity on who was doing the undue influence, that this report was overboard and unnecessary and that the fees incurred to keep fighting this case should be surcharged. Ms. Harrington stated that it happens sometimes that people find a fiduciary not to be a good fit, that Mr. Haslett asked Mr. Lucas to return his property and that he would choose a new trustee and Mr. Lucas refused, that it is dangerous that a fiduciary could refuse without a court order to return assets without a very expensive report and that Mr. Haslett is being forced to pay for extra counsel. The Petition to Compel Trustee to Deliver Assets of Surviving Spouse Trust and Demand for Accounting was continued to December 5, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Chan. (Not Reported)

Petition To Approve Account And Report Of Successor Trustee Thomas A. Lucas And For Approval Of Payment Of Successor Trustees Fees
Off Calendar Sep-28-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Boessenecker for (objector) Davis Haslett. Daniel Conrad for and with (petitioner) Thomas Lucas.

The Court asked where the parties stand. Mr. Conrad stated that this is Mr. Lucas' accounting petition and request for approval of fees, that it is not the same as Mr. Haslett's petition, that Mr. Lucas appreciates the analysis by Dr. Canick, that at the hearing in two weeks they will inform the Court, that they are asking today for approval of fees, that they accounting was underway at the same time as Mr. Haslett's petition, that some securities were sold, that some had declined in value before Mr. Lucas became trustee, that there has been no material objection to the accounting and that Mr. Lucas has done an excellent job with a difficult case. The Court asked if there would be an amendment to the accounting. Mr. Conrad stated that the accounting ended on 2/29/16, that it is totally separate and that it will not affect Mr. Haslett's petition. Mr. Boessenecker stated that it is to Mr. Lucas' advantage to have the accounting granted today, that the petition set for 9/21/16 does have to do with this petition, that they have asked for an itemized statement of Mr. Lucas' fees, that there is no reflection in the papers of attorney's fees, that in regards to Dr. Canick's report there is a bill for $15,000.00 that should be paid by Mr. Lucas, that Dr. Canick confirmed what had already been told to the Court and that Mr. Haslett asked Mr. Lucas to resign in November. The Court asked if the petition is taken under submission as of 9/21/16 what would it affect. Mr. Boessenecker stated that if the Court does not need Mr. Lucas' itemized statement he thinks that surcharges are appropriate and that Mr. Lucas insisted that Dr. Canick review a 2000 page Kaiser report which accounts for much of the fee. Mr. Conrad stated that Mr. Haslett has Parkinson's and Dementia, that Mr. Lucas had been asking for an evaluation since October of 2015, that everything in the 9/21/16 petition is post accounting and that there are no material objections. The Court took the Petition to Approve Account and Report of Successor Trustee Thomas A. Lucas and for Approval of Payment of Successor Trustees Fees under submission as of September 21, 2016 and stated that any additional documents must be filed no later than September 14, 2016.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Fender. (Not Reported)
